bias_direction: Renaming a namespace across a signed-off governance doc under an owner one-liner ('fix it') risks scope creep and the appearance of tidiness-for-its-own-sake; the author also had an incentive to bound the sweep narrowly (definitional home + active surfaces) to keep the change tractable in one turn, which could be read as under-delivering on a directive that sounded total ('that needs to change').
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer could object that renaming the entity side while leaving ~250 item_code references and all historical mentions untouched produces a repo where E-## still appears everywhere, so the collision is only half-resolved. The counter: item_codes are the other, correct namespace (renaming them is a high-risk data migration, not a fix), and append-only records (sessions, archived, dated audits, prior DRs) are immutable by the repo's own forward-only convention; the collision is resolved where it is defined and actively read, and a namespace note now makes the split explicit. Whether to sweep remaining active references/ mentions is a bounded follow-up, not a blocker.
